oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Incorporating technology in chuteside diagnostics
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: White, Brad
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: cattle
diagnostics
bovine respiratory disease
prognosis
chuteside tools
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Chuteside decisions are one of the most critical and challengôÙing aspects of cattle health management. Diseased cattle may display similar clinical signs, but may be suffering from differôÙent pathologic processes including bronchopneumonia, acute interstitial pneumonia, and congestive heart failure. The objecôÙtive of this review is to describe integration of new technologies and approaches to improve diagnostic precision, prioritize case management, and more accurately generate individual case prognosis. Economic research illustrates the importance of difôÙferentiating among recoverable and non-recoverable cases. ApôÙpropriate expectations for case outcomes should guide chuteside evaluations. Specific evaluation techniques are explored includôÙing cardiac troponin, hydration status, targeted thoracic ultraôÙsound, facial pictures and predictive models. Individual diagôÙnostic modalities illustrate promise, and using information from multiple sources may lead to the best decisions. This summary illustrates chuteside technologies may assist in refining case defiôÙnitions which could lead to more customized management.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 29-30
